title: sophora arizonica var. formosa
intro: Sophora arizonica var. formosa, also known as Mexican root or Arizona sophora, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its isoflavone content, particularly sophoranol and isocorydine, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sophora arizonica var. formosa include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for use in herbal medicine, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and optimal usage.
